Today three things are wrong: + +1. **The field icon is outside the field's ``**, so clicking the icon does not focus the field, and the focus bar paints between the icon and the field content rather than to the left of the icon. +2. **Up / Down arrow nav between sibling fields is unreliable** (likely the rects-on-scroll bug `01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y` plus inspector panel scrolling). +3. **Enter on a pill / badge-list field doesn't drill into the pills.** `01KQ9X3A9NMRYK50GWP4S4ZMJ4` introduces a scope-level `field.edit` bound to Enter that always enters edit mode — but for fields with spatial children (pills), Enter should drill in to focus the first pill, then arrow-nav among pills. + +This ticket fixes all three so a user clicking, arrow-keying, and pressing Enter on inspector fields gets the experience the project promised. + +## Where the bugs live + +### Bug 1 — Icon outside the focus zone + +`kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.tsx:325–333` (`FieldRow`): + +```jsx +
+ {Icon && } +
{content}
+
+``` + +The icon sits as a sibling of `` (which renders ``). `` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx:410`) wraps its contents in `...` — so the focus zone covers only the content branch, not the icon. Consequences: + +- A click on ``'s icon does not bubble to any `` / ``, so spatial focus does not move to the field. The user reports this directly. +- The `` paints `-left-2` from the field zone's left edge — i.e. somewhere between the icon and the content. The user wants the bar to the LEFT of the icon. + +The icon can't be added to `` unconditionally (the icon is inspector-specific — it's not rendered for `` in card cells or grid cells). The right fix is to **lift the focus zone responsibility one level up** to the FieldRow, so the row's outer `
` IS the FocusZone, and the icon and content both sit inside it. `` continues to own its FocusZone in its other consumers (card, grid, navbar) — only inspector use is restructured. + +Two implementation options, decided by the implementer based on the failing tests: + +- **Option A** — give `` an opt-out (`wrap?: boolean` or similar) that suppresses its internal `` + `` wrap. `` then renders `` icon + `` ``. Single source of `field:...` zone identity per use site. +- **Option B** — render the icon inside `` when `mode="full"`. `` looks up `fieldDef.icon` + the display's `iconOverride` itself (it has `value` already) and renders the icon as the first child inside its existing ``. `` then drops the row wrapper and renders just ``. + +Option B keeps Field as the single seam for the field zone but couples Field to icon-rendering logic that today lives in `entity-inspector.tsx`. Option A keeps Field a pure data-binding component but introduces a `wrap` prop. Pick whichever the failing tests point at — both produce the same observable user behavior. + +### Bug 2 — Vertical nav between fields + +The inspector panel is a `` containing a stack of field zones. Beam search "down" from one field zone should pick the next field zone by rect. This requires accurate rects on every field zone, which today are stale when the inspector panel scrolls (the inspector body is scrollable — see `slide-panel.tsx:45` `overflow-y-auto`). + +The rects-on-scroll fix in `01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y` (this ticket's dependency) addresses the registration side. Once that lands, vertical nav should work for inspector fields. This ticket pins the contract via tests so the inspector-specific case is verified, not assumed. + +### Bug 3 — Enter on pill fields must drill in, not edit + +`01KQ9X3A9NMRYK50GWP4S4ZMJ4` introduces `field.edit` bound to Enter at the field-zone scope level. That shadows the global `nav.drillIn` for every focused field zone. For fields whose display renders pills (`badge-list-display.tsx` → `MentionView` → each pill is a `` leaf inside the field zone — see `mention-view.tsx:263–272`), the user wants Enter to drill in to the first pill, then arrow-nav among pills (in-zone beam search), then Escape to drill back to the field zone. + +The current `field.edit` always calls `onEdit`; for a pill field, `onEdit` opens an editor where applicable, which is not what the user wants — they want to navigate the existing pills. + +Fix: change `field.edit`'s execute closure to **drill in first, then edit only on null kernel result**: + +```ts +{ + id: "field.edit", + name: "Edit Field", + keys: { vim: "Enter", cua: "Enter" }, + execute: async () => { + // Pills (or any spatial children) win — drill into them. + const moniker = await actions.drillIn(fieldZoneKey); + if (moniker !== null) { + setFocus(moniker); + return; + } + // No spatial children — fall through to edit mode. + onEdit?.(); + }, +} +``` + +This unifies the two behaviours: a field with pills drills in (kernel returns the first pill), a field without pills enters edit mode (kernel returns null, fallback fires). The same scope-level command serves both — no per-display branching at registration time. + +For non-editable fields with no pills (`onEdit` undefined, no spatial children), Enter is a no-op — consistent with "leaves with no editor have nothing to drill into". + +## Acceptance Criteria + +All asserted by automated tests below — no manual smoke step. + +### Click + focus bar (Bug 1) + +- [ ] Clicking a field row's **icon** inside the inspector focuses the field zone. `useFocusedScope()` reports the field's moniker, the `` renders, and `data-focused="true"` flips on the field zone's wrapper. +- [ ] Clicking a field row's **content** (label, value, pill area) inside the inspector focuses the field zone. Same assertions. +- [ ] The rendered `` element is positioned to the **left of the icon**, not between the icon and the content. Asserted via DOM order: the indicator's host is the field-zone wrapper, the icon is the first child of that wrapper, and the indicator's `-left-2` offset places it before the icon's left edge. +- [ ] No regression: `` rendered in a card cell or grid cell still wraps in its own `` (Option A) or its inspector mode adds the icon inline (Option B); either way, the existing card / grid / navbar callers continue to work without code changes. + +### Up / Down nav (Bug 2) + +- [ ] `nav.down` from a focused field zone in the inspector lands on the next field zone in document order. +- [ ] `nav.up` from a focused field zone lands on the previous field zone. +- [ ] After scrolling the inspector panel body, `nav.down` and `nav.up` continue to pick the correct sibling. (Regression guard against the rects-on-scroll bug; depends on `01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`.) +- [ ] `nav.down` from the last visible field zone scrolls the inspector body to bring the next field into view, then settles focus on that field. (Mirrors the column-view pattern.) + +### Enter drill-in semantics (Bug 3) + +- [ ] Enter on a focused field zone whose display renders **pills** drills into the first pill — `useFocusedScope()` reports the first pill's moniker, and the field stays in display mode (does not enter edit mode). +- [ ] After drilling into pills, `nav.right` from the first pill lands on the second pill; `nav.left` walks back; arrow nav works in both directions across all pills in the field. +- [ ] Escape from a focused pill drills out to the field zone (existing drill-out chain). +- [ ] Enter on a focused field zone whose display has **no spatial children** and is editable enters edit mode — `editing` flips to `true`, `` mounts, DOM focus lands on the editor input. (Regression guard for the existing `field.edit` from `01KQ9X3A9NMRYK50GWP4S4ZMJ4`.) +- [ ] Enter on a focused field zone whose display has no spatial children AND is non-editable is a no-op — no edit mode, no inspector dispatch, no error. + +### Side effects guarded + +- [ ] Double-click on the field zone (icon or content) still dispatches `ui.inspect` for the field's moniker (regression guard for the inspector-on-dblclick contract). +- [ ] Space on the focused field zone dispatches `ui.inspect` (depends on `01KQ9XJ4XGKVW24EZSQCA6K3E2` Space-on-Inspectable; if not landed yet, this assertion can be deferred to that ticket). + +## Tests + +All tests are automated. No manual verification. + +### Frontend — `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.field-click-icon.browser.test.tsx` (new file) + +Mounts the inspector panel for a task inside the production provider stack against the per-test backend. + +- [ ] `clicking_field_icon_focuses_field_zone` — render an inspector field with a `field.icon` set in the YAML, click the rendered icon element, assert `useFocusedScope()` reports `field:task:T1.` and the field zone wrapper has `data-focused="true"`. +- [ ] `clicking_field_content_focuses_field_zone` — same as above but click on the content (label / value), same assertions. +- [ ] `focus_indicator_appears_to_left_of_icon` — focus the field zone via `spatial_focus(key)`, assert the rendered `[data-testid="focus-indicator"]` is a child of the field-zone wrapper AND the icon is rendered AFTER it in DOM order. (The bar's `-left-2` means it visually paints to the left of the wrapper's content — the structural assertion is that the icon is INSIDE the wrapper, not a sibling.) +- [ ] `field_in_card_cell_unaffected` — render `` in a card cell consumer (not the inspector), assert the existing FocusZone-on-Field structure is preserved (regression guard for card/grid callers). + +Test command: `bun run test:browser entity-inspector.field-click-icon.browser.test.tsx` — all four pass. + +### Frontend — `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.field-vertical-nav.browser.test.tsx` (new file) + +- [ ] `down_from_first_field_lands_on_second_field` — focus the first inspector field, fire `keydown { key: "ArrowDown" }`, assert `useFocusedScope()` reports the second field's moniker. +- [ ] `up_from_last_field_lands_on_previous_field` — symmetric. +- [ ] `down_after_scroll_picks_next_field_in_content_order` — render an inspector with enough fields to require panel scrolling; scroll the inspector body; press ArrowDown; assert the next field gains focus. (Depends on `01KQ9XBAG5P9W3JREQYNGAYM8Y`.) +- [ ] `down_at_last_visible_field_scrolls_to_bring_next_field_into_view` — focus the last on-screen field, ArrowDown, assert the panel scrolled and focus landed on the now-visible next field. + +Test command: `bun run test:browser entity-inspector.field-vertical-nav.browser.test.tsx` — all four pass. + +### Frontend — `kanban-app/ui/src/components/entity-inspector.field-enter-drill.browser.test.tsx` (new file) + +Builds an inspector for a task with both pill fields (e.g. `tags`) and editable scalar fields (e.g. `name`). + +- [ ] `enter_on_pill_field_drills_into_first_pill` — focus the tags field zone, fire `keydown { key: "Enter" }`, assert `useFocusedScope()` reports the first pill's moniker (e.g. `tag:`) and `editing` on the field is still `false`. +- [ ] `right_from_first_pill_lands_on_second_pill` — after drilling in, fire ArrowRight, assert focus moves to the next pill. +- [ ] `escape_from_pill_drills_back_to_field_zone` — fire Escape on the focused pill, assert focus returns to the field zone moniker (existing drill-out chain). +- [ ] `enter_on_editable_scalar_field_enters_edit_mode` — focus the name field, Enter, assert `editing` flipped to `true` and `document.activeElement` is the editor input. (Regression guard for `01KQ9X3A9NMRYK50GWP4S4ZMJ4`.) +- [ ] `enter_on_non_editable_field_with_no_pills_is_noop` — focus a `editor: "none"` field with no display children, Enter, assert nothing changes — no edit, no dispatch, no focus move. +- [ ] `enter_on_pill_field_with_zero_pills_falls_through_to_edit_or_noop` — when the field is editable but the value is empty (no pills rendered), Enter falls through to edit mode (if `onEdit` is wired) or is a no-op (otherwise).
